In kube-scheduler, after\\n39: \\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\\n40: \\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\\n41: \\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\\n42: \\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\\n43: \\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\\n44: \\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\\n45: \\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\\n46: \\tmetav1.TypeMeta\\n47: \\n48: \\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\\n49: \\tParallelism int32\\n50: \\n51: \\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\\n52: \\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\\n53: \\n54: \\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\\n55: \\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\\n56: \\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\\n57: \\n58: \\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\\n59: \\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n60: \\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n61: \\n62: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n63: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n64: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n65: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n66: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n67: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n68: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n69: \\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\\n70: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n71: \\n72: \\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n73: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n74: \\t// will be used.\\n75: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\\n76: \\n77: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n78: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n79: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n80: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\\n81: \\n82: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\\n83: \\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\\n84: \\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n85: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\tExtenders []Extender\\n91: \\n92: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n93: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n94: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n95: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n96: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n101: \\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\\n102: \\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \\\"spec.schedulerName\\\", then the pod\\n103: \\t// is scheduled with this profile.\\n104: \\tSchedulerName string\\n105: \\n106: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n107: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n108: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n109: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n110: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n111: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n112: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n113: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n114: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n115: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n118: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n119: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n120: \\t// should be disabled.\\n121: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n122: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n123: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n124: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n125: \\tPlugins *Plugins\\n126: \\n127: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n128: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n129: \\t// for that plugin.\\n130: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n138: type Plugins struct {\\n139: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n140: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\\n141: \\n142: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n143: \\tQueueSort PluginSet\\n144: \\n145: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n146: \\tPreFilter PluginSet\\n147: \\n148: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n149: \\tFilter PluginSet\\n150: \\n151: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n152: \\tPostFilter PluginSet\\n153: \\n154: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n155: \\tPreScore PluginSet\\n156: \\n157: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n158: \\tScore PluginSet\\n159: \\n160: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n161: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n162: \\tReserve PluginSet\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n165: \\tPermit PluginSet\\n166: \\n167: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n168: \\tPreBind PluginSet\\n169: \\n170: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n171: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n172: \\tBind PluginSet\\n173: \\n174: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n175: \\tPostBind PluginSet\\n176: \\n177: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\\n178: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n183: type PluginSet struct {\\n184: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n185: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n186: \\tEnabled []Plugin\\n187: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n188: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n189: \\tDisabled []Plugin\\n190: }\\n191: \\n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n193: type Plugin struct {\\n194: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n195: \\tName string\\n196: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n197: \\tWeight int32\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\\n204: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n205: \\tName string\\n206: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n207: \\tArgs runtime.Object\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: /*\\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\\n212:  */\\n213: const (\\n214: \\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\\n215: \\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\\n216: \\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\\n217: \\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\\n218: \\n219: \\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\\n220: \\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\\n221: \\n222: \\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\\n223: \\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n224: \\n225: \\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\\n226: \\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\\n227: )\\n228: \\n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\\n231: \\tif p == nil {\\n232: \\t\\treturn nil\\n233: \\t}\\n234: \\textensions := []PluginSet{\\n235: \\t\\tp.PreEnqueue,\\n236: \\t\\tp.PreFilter,\\n237: \\t\\tp.Filter,\\n238: \\t\\tp.PostFilter,\\n239: \\t\\tp.Reserve,\\n240: \\t\\tp.PreScore,\\n241: \\t\\tp.Score,\\n242: \\t\\tp.PreBind,\\n243: \\t\\tp.Bind,\\n244: \\t\\tp.PostBind,\\n245: \\t\\tp.Permit,\\n246: \\t\\tp.QueueSort,\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tn := sets.New[string]()\\n249: \\tfor _, e := range extensions {\\n250: \\t\\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\\n251: \\t\\t\\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn sets.List(n)\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n259: type Extender struct {\\n260: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n261: \\tURLPrefix string\\n262: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n263: \\tFilterVerb string\\n264: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n265: \\tPreemptVerb string\\n266: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n267: \\tPrioritizeVerb string\\n268: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n269: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n270: \\tWeight int64\\n271: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n272: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n273: \\t// can implement this function.\\n274: \\tBindVerb string\\n275: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n276: \\tEnableHTTPS bool\\n277: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n278: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\\n279: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\\n280: \\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\\n281: \\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\\n282: \\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\\n283: \\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\\n284: \\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\\n285: \\tNodeCacheCapable bool\\n286: \\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\\n287: \\t// this extender.\\n288: \\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\\n289: \\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\\n290: \\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\\n291: \\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\\n292: \\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\\n293: \\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\\n294: \\t// +optional\\n295: \\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\\n296: \\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\\n297: \\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\\n298: \\tIgnorable bool\\n299: }\\n300: \\n301: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\\n302: // managed by an extender.\\n303: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\\n304: \\t// Name is the extended resource name.\\n305: \\tName string\\n306: \\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\\n307: \\t// resource when applying predicates.\\n308: \\tIgnoredByScheduler bool\\n309: }\\n310: \\n311: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\\n312: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\\n313: \\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\\n314: \\tInsecure bool\\n315: \\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\\n316: \\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\\n317: \\t// server is used.\\n318: \\tServerName string\\n319: \\n320: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n321: \\tCertFile string\\n322: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n323: \\tKeyFile string\\n324: \\t// Trusted root certificates for server\\n325: \\tCAFile string\\n326: \\n327: \\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\\n328: \\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\\n329: \\tCertData []byte\\n330: \\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\\n331: \\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\\n332: \\tKeyData []byte `datapolicy:\\\"security-key\\\"`\\n333: \\t// CAData \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n25: \\tcomponentbaseconfig \\\"k8s.io/component-base/config\\\"\\n26: )\\n27: \\n28: const (\\n29: \\t// DefaultKubeSchedulerPort is the default port for the scheduler status server.\\n30: \\t// May be overridden by a flag at startup.\\n31: \\tDefaultKubeSchedulerPort = 10259\\n32: )\\n33: \\n34: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n35: \\n36: // KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\\n37: type KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\\n38: \\t// TypeMeta contains the API version and kind. In kube-scheduler, after\\n39: \\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\\n40: \\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\\n41: \\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\\n42: \\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\\n43: \\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\\n44: \\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\\n45: \\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\\n46: \\tmetav1.TypeMeta\\n47: \\n48: \\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\\n49: \\tParallelism int32\\n50: \\n51: \\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\\n52: \\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\\n53: \\n54: \\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\\n55: \\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\\n56: \\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\\n57: \\n58: \\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\\n59: \\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n60: \\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n61: \\n62: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n63: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n64: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n65: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n66: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n67: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n68: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n69: \\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\\n70: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n71: \\n72: \\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n73: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n74: \\t// will be used.\\n75: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\\n76: \\n77: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n78: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n79: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n80: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\\n81: \\n82: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\\n83: \\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\\n84: \\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n85: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\tExtenders []Extender\\n91: \\n92: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n93: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n94: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n95: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n96: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n101: \\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\\n102: \\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \\\"spec.schedulerName\\\", then the pod\\n103: \\t// is scheduled with this profile.\\n104: \\tSchedulerName string\\n105: \\n106: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n107: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n108: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n109: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n110: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n111: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n112: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n113: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n114: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n115: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n118: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n119: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n120: \\t// should be disabled.\\n121: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n122: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n123: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n124: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n125: \\tPlugins *Plugins\\n126: \\n127: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n128: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n129: \\t// for that plugin.\\n130: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n138: type Plugins struct {\\n139: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n140: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\\n141: \\n142: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n143: \\tQueueSort PluginSet\\n144: \\n145: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n146: \\tPreFilter PluginSet\\n147: \\n148: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n149: \\tFilter PluginSet\\n150: \\n151: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n152: \\tPostFilter PluginSet\\n153: \\n154: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n155: \\tPreScore PluginSet\\n156: \\n157: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n158: \\tScore PluginSet\\n159: \\n160: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n161: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n162: \\tReserve PluginSet\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n165: \\tPermit PluginSet\\n166: \\n167: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n168: \\tPreBind PluginSet\\n169: \\n170: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n171: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n172: \\tBind PluginSet\\n173: \\n174: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n175: \\tPostBind PluginSet\\n176: \\n177: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\\n178: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n183: type PluginSet struct {\\n184: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n185: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n186: \\tEnabled []Plugin\\n187: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n188: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n189: \\tDisabled []Plugin\\n190: }\\n191: \\n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n193: type Plugin struct {\\n194: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n195: \\tName string\\n196: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n197: \\tWeight int32\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\\n204: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n205: \\tName string\\n206: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n207: \\tArgs runtime.Object\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: /*\\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\\n212:  */\\n213: const (\\n214: \\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\\n215: \\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\\n216: \\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\\n217: \\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\\n218: \\n219: \\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\\n220: \\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\\n221: \\n222: \\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\\n223: \\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n224: \\n225: \\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\\n226: \\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\\n227: )\\n228: \\n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\\n231: \\tif p == nil {\\n232: \\t\\treturn nil\\n233: \\t}\\n234: \\textensions := []PluginSet{\\n235: \\t\\tp.PreEnqueue,\\n236: \\t\\tp.PreFilter,\\n237: \\t\\tp.Filter,\\n238: \\t\\tp.PostFilter,\\n239: \\t\\tp.Reserve,\\n240: \\t\\tp.PreScore,\\n241: \\t\\tp.Score,\\n242: \\t\\tp.PreBind,\\n243: \\t\\tp.Bind,\\n244: \\t\\tp.PostBind,\\n245: \\t\\tp.Permit,\\n246: \\t\\tp.QueueSort,\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tn := sets.New[string]()\\n249: \\tfor _, e := range extensions {\\n250: \\t\\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\\n251: \\t\\t\\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn sets.List(n)\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n259: type Extender struct {\\n260: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n261: \\tURLPrefix string\\n262: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n263: \\tFilterVerb string\\n264: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n265: \\tPreemptVerb string\\n266: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n267: \\tPrioritizeVerb string\\n268: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n269: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n270: \\tWeight int64\\n271: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n272: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n273: \\t// can implement this function.\\n274: \\tBindVerb string\\n275: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n276: \\tEnableHTTPS bool\\n277: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n278: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\\n279: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n90: \tExtenders []Extender\n91: \n92: \t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n93: \t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n94: \t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n95: \t// Defaults to false.\n96: \tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n97: }\n98: \n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n101: \t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n102: \t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n103: \t// is scheduled with this profile.\n104: \tSchedulerName string\n105: \n106: \t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n107: \t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n108: \t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n109: \t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n110: \t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n111: \t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n112: \t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n113: \t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n114: \t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n115: \tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n116: \n117: \t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n118: \t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n119: \t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n120: \t// should be disabled.\n121: \t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n122: \t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n123: \t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n124: \t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n125: \tPlugins *Plugins\n126: \n127: \t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n128: \t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n129: \t// for that plugin.\n130: \tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n131: }\n132: \n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n138: type Plugins struct {\n139: \t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n140: \tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n141: \n142: \t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n143: \tQueueSort PluginSet\n144: \n145: \t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n146: \tPreFilter PluginSet\n147: \n148: \t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n149: \tFilter PluginSet\n150: \n151: \t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n152: \tPostFilter PluginSet\n153: \n154: \t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n155: \tPreScore PluginSet\n156: \n157: \t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n158: \tScore PluginSet\n159: \n160: \t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n161: \t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n162: \tReserve PluginSet\n163: \n164: \t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n165: \tPermit PluginSet\n166: \n167: \t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n168: \tPreBind PluginSet\n169: \n170: \t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n171: \t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n172: \tBind PluginSet\n173: \n174: \t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n175: \tPostBind PluginSet\n176: \n177: \t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n178: \tMultiPoint PluginSet\n179: }\n180: \n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n183: type PluginSet struct {\n184: \t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n185: \t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n186: \tEnabled []Plugin\n187: \t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n188: \t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n189: \tDisabled []Plugin\n190: }\n191: \n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\n193: type Plugin struct {\n194: \t// Name defines the name of plugin\n195: \tName string\n196: \t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n197: \tWeight int32\n198: }\n199: \n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\n204: \t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n205: \tName string\n206: \t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n207: \tArgs runtime.Object\n208: }\n209: \n210: /*\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n212:  */\n213: const (\n214: \t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n215: \t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n216: \t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n217: \tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n218: \n219: \t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n220: \tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n221: \n222: \t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n223: \tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n224: \n225: \t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n226: \tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n227: )\n228: \n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n231: \tif p == nil {\n232: \t\treturn nil\n233: \t}\n234: \textensions := []PluginSet{\n235: \t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n236: \t\tp.PreFilter,\n237: \t\tp.Filter,\n238: \t\tp.PostFilter,\n239: \t\tp.Reserve,\n240: \t\tp.PreScore,\n241: \t\tp.Score,\n242: \t\tp.PreBind,\n243: \t\tp.Bind,\n244: \t\tp.PostBind,\n245: \t\tp.Permit,\n246: \t\tp.QueueSort,\n247: \t}\n248: \tn := sets.New[string]()\n249: \tfor _, e := range extensions {\n250: \t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n251: \t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n252: \t\t}\n253: \t}\n254: \treturn sets.List(n)\n255: }\n256: \n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\n259: type Extender struct {\n260: \t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n261: \tURLPrefix string\n262: \t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n263: \tFilterVerb string\n264: \t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n265: \tPreemptVerb string\n266: \t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n267: \tPrioritizeVerb string\n268: \t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n269: \t// The weight should be a positive integer\n270: \tWeight int64\n271: \t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n272: \t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n273: \t// can implement this function.\n274: \tBindVerb string\n275: \t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n276: \tEnableHTTPS bool\n277: \t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n278: \tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n279: \t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n280: \t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n281: \tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\n282: \t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n283: \t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n284: \t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n285: \tNodeCacheCapable bool\n286: \t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n287: \t// this extender.\n288: \t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n289: \t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n290: \t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n291: \t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n292: \t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n293: \t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n294: \t// +optional\n295: \tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\n296: \t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n297: \t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n298: \tIgnorable bool\n299: }\n300: \n301: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n302: // managed by an extender.\n303: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n304: \t// Name is the extended resource name.\n305: \tName string\n306: \t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n307: \t// resource when applying predicates.\n308: \tIgnoredByScheduler bool\n309: }\n310: \n311: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\n312: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n313: \t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n314: \tInsecure bool\n315: \t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n316: \t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\n317: \t// server is used.\n318: \tServerName string\n319: \n320: \t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n321: \tCertFile string\n322: \t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n323: \tKeyFile string\n324: \t// Trusted root certificates for server\n325: \tCAFile string\n326: \n327: \t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\n328: \t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\n329: \tCertData []byte\n330: \t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\n331: \t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\n332: \tKeyData []byte `datapolicy:\"security-key\"`\n333: \t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\n334: \t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\n335: \tCAData []byte\n336: }\n337: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/types.go"}}}

to schedule pods\\\")\\n61: \\n62: // Scheduler watches for new unscheduled pods. It attempts to find\\n63: // nodes that they fit on and writes bindings back to the api server.\\n64: type Scheduler struct {\\n65: \\t// It is expected that changes made via Cache will be observed\\n66: \\t// by NodeLister and Algorithm.\\n67: \\tCache internalcache.Cache\\n68: \\n69: \\tExtenders []framework.Extender\\n70: \\n71: \\t// NextPod should be a function that blocks until the next pod\\n72: \\t// is available. We don't use a channel for this, because scheduling\\n73: \\t// a pod may take some amount of time and we don't want pods to get\\n74: \\t// stale while they sit in a channel.\\n75: \\tNextPod func(logger klog.Logger) (*framework.QueuedPodInfo, error)\\n76: \\n77: \\t// FailureHandler is called upon a scheduling failure.\\n78: \\tFailureHandler FailureHandlerFn\\n79: \\n80: \\t// SchedulePod tries to schedule the given pod to one of the nodes in the node list.\\n81: \\t// Return a struct of ScheduleResult with the name of suggested host on success,\\n82: \\t// otherwise will return a FitError with reasons.\\n83: \\tSchedulePod func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (ScheduleResult, error)\\n84: \\n85: \\t// Close this to shut down the scheduler.\\n86: \\tStopEverything <-chan struct{}\\n87: \\n88: \\t// SchedulingQueue holds pods to be scheduled\\n89: \\tSchedulingQueue internalqueue.SchedulingQueue\\n90: \\n91: \\t// Profiles are the scheduling profiles.\\n92: \\tProfiles profile.Map\\n93: \\n94: \\tclient clientset.Interface\\n95: \\n96: \\tnodeInfoSnapshot *internalcache.Snapshot\\n97: \\n98: \\tpercentageOfNodesToScore int32\\n99: \\n100: \\tnextStartNodeIndex int\\n101: \\n102: \\t// logger *must* be initialized when creating a Scheduler,\\n103: \\t// otherwise logging functions will access a nil sink and\\n104: \\t// panic.\\n105: \\tlogger klog.Logger\\n106: \\n107: \\t// registeredHandlers contains the registrations of all handlers. It's used to check if all handlers have finished syncing before the scheduling cycles start.\\n108: \\tregisteredHandlers []cache.ResourceEventHandlerRegistration\\n109: }\\n110: \\n111: func (sched *Scheduler) applyDefaultHandlers() {\\n112: \\tsched.SchedulePod = sched.schedulePod\\n113: \\tsched.FailureHandler = sched.handleSchedulingFailure\\n114: }\\n115: \\n116: type schedulerOptions struct }\\n149: \\n150: // WithComponentConfigVersion sets the component config version to the\\n151: // KubeSchedulerConfiguration version used. The string should be the full\\n152: // scheme group/version of the external type we converted from (for example\\n153: // \\\"kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\\\")\\n154: func WithComponentConfigVersion(apiVersion string) Option {\\n155: \\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\\n156: \\t\\to.componentConfigVersion = apiVersion\\n157: \\t}\\n158: }\\n159: \\n160: // WithKubeConfig sets the kube config for Scheduler.\\n161: func WithKubeConfig(cfg *restclient.Config) Option {\\n162: \\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\\n163: \\t\\to.kubeConfig = cfg\\n164: \\t}\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // WithProfiles sets profiles for Scheduler. By default, there is one profile\\n168: // with the name \\\"default-scheduler\\\".\\n169: func WithProfiles(p ...schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile) Option {\\n170: \\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\\n171: \\t\\to.profiles = p\\n172: \\t\\to.applyDefaultProfile = false\\n173: \\t}\\n174: }\\n175: \\n176: // WithParallelism sets the parallelism for all scheduler algorithms. \\t// from the SchedulingQueue.\\n442: \\t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\\n443: \\t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\\n444: \\t// profiles[0].Plugins != nil {\n215: \t\tcanon = profiles[0].Plugins.QueueSort\n216: \t\tif len(profiles[0].Plugins.QueueSort.Enabled) != 0 {\n217: \t\t\tqueueSortName = profiles[0].Plugins.QueueSort.Enabled[0].Name\n218: \t\t}\n219: \t\tlength := len(profiles[0].Plugins.QueueSort.Enabled)\n220: \t\tif length > 1 {\n221: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, field.Invalid(path.Index(0).Child(\"plugins\", \"queueSort\", \"Enabled\"), length, \"only one queue sort plugin can be enabled\"))\n222: \t\t}\n223: \t}\n224: \tfor _, cfg := range profiles[0].PluginConfig {\n225: \t\tif len(queueSortName) > 0 && cfg.Name == queueSortName {\n226: \t\t\tqueueSortArgs = cfg.Args\n227: \t\t}\n228: \t}\n229: \tfor i := 1; i < len(profiles); i++ {\n230: \t\tvar curr config.PluginSet\n231: \t\tif profiles[i].Plugins != nil {\n232: \t\t\tcurr = profiles[i].Plugins.QueueSort\n233: \t\t}\n234: \t\tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(canon, curr) {\n235: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, field.Invalid(path.Index(i).Child(\"plugins\", \"queueSort\"), curr, \"queueSort must be the same for all profiles\"))\n236: \t\t}\n237: \t\tfor _, cfg := range profiles[i].PluginConfig {\n238: \t\t\tif cfg.Name == queueSortName && !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(queueSortArgs, cfg.Args) {\n239: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, field.Invalid(path.Index(i).Child(\"pluginConfig\", \"args\"), cfg.Args, \"queueSort must be the same for all profiles\"))\n240: \t\t\t}\n241: \t\t}\n242: \t}\n243: \treturn errs\n244: }\n245: \n246: // validateExtenders validates the configured extenders for the Scheduler\n247: func validateExtenders(fldPath *field.Path, extenders []config.Extender) []error {\n248: \tvar errs []error\n249: \tbinders := 0\n250: \textenderManagedResources := sets.New[string]()\n251: \tfor i, extender := range extenders {\n252: \t\tpath := fldPath.Index(i)\n253: \t\tif len(extender.PrioritizeVerb) > 0 && extender.Weight <= 0 {\n254: \t\t\terrs = append(errs, field.Invalid(path.Child(\"weight\"),\n255: \t\t\t\textender.Weight, \"must have a positive weight applied to it\"))\n256: \t\t}\n257: \t\tif extender.BindVerb != \"\" {\n258: \t\t\tbinders++\n259: \t\t}\n260: \t\tfor j, resource := range extender.ManagedResources {\n261: \t\t\tmanagedResourcesPath := path.Child(\"managedResources\").Index(j)\n262: \t\t\tvalidationErrors := validateExtendedResourceName(managedResourcesPath.Child(\"name\"), v1.ResourceName(resource.Name))\n263: \t\t\terrs = \t\t\t\t\tresource.Name, \"duplicate extender managed resource name\"))\n267: \t\t\t}\n268: \t\t\textenderManagedResources.Insert(resource.Name)\n269: \t\t}\n270: \t}\n271: \tif binders > 1 {\n272: \t\terrs = append(errs, field.Invalid(fldPath, fmt.Sprintf(\"found %d extenders implementing bind\", binders), \"only one extender can implement bind\"))\n273: \t}\n274: \treturn errs\n275: }\n276: \n277: // validateExtendedResourceName checks whether the specified name is a valid\n278: // extended resource name.\n279: func validateExtendedResourceName(path *field.Path, name v1.ResourceName) []error {\n280: \tvar validationErrors []error\n281: \tfor _, msg := range validation.IsQualifiedName(string(name)) {\n282: \t\tvalidationErrors = append(validationErrors, field.Invalid(path, name, msg))\n283: \t}\n284: \tif len(validationErrors) != 0 {\n285: \t\treturn validationErrors\n286: \t}\n287: \tif !v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(name) {\n288: \t\tvalidationErrors = append(validationErrors, field.Invalid(path, string(name), \"is an invalid extended resource name\"))\n289: \t}\n290: \treturn validationErrors\n291: }\n292: {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_019KNZvn79k66qY3VWTEombL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019qSQFH5DkAQBUakb9U25a9","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/types.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage config\n\nimport (\n\t\"math\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\tcomponentbaseconfig \"k8s.io/component-base/config\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// DefaultKubeSchedulerPort is the default port for the scheduler status server.\n\t// May be overridden by a flag at startup.\n\tDefaultKubeSchedulerPort = 10259\n)\n\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n\n// KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\ntype KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\n\t// TypeMeta contains the API version and kind. In kube-scheduler, after\n\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\n\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\n\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\n\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\n\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\n\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\n\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta\n\n\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\n\tParallelism int32\n\n\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\n\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\n\n\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\n\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\n\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\n\n\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\n\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n\n\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\n\t// will be used.\n\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\n\n\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\n\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\n\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\n\n\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\n\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\n\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n\t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n\tProfiles []SchedulingProfile\n\n\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n\tExtenders []Extender\n\n\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n\t// Defaults to false.\n\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n}\n\n// SchedulingProfile is a scheduling profile.\ntype SchedulingProfile struct {\n\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n\tSchedulerName string\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n\n\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n\t// should be disabled.\n\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n\tPlugins *Plugins\n\n\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n\t// for that plugin.\n\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n}\n\n// KubeSchedulerProfile is an alias for SchedulingProfile for backwards compatibility.\n// Deprecated: Use SchedulingProfile instead.\ntype KubeSchedulerProfile = SchedulingProfile\n\n// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\ntype Plugins struct {\n\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n\n\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n\tQueueSort PluginSet\n\n\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\tPreFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n\tFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n\tPostFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n\tPreScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n\tScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n\tReserve PluginSet\n\n\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n\tPermit PluginSet\n\n\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n\tPreBind PluginSet\n\n\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n\tBind PluginSet\n\n\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n\tPostBind PluginSet\n\n\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n\tMultiPoint PluginSet\n}\n\n// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\ntype PluginSet struct {\n\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n\tEnabled []Plugin\n\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n\tDisabled []Plugin\n}\n\n// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\ntype Plugin struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n\tName string\n\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n\tWeight int32\n}\n\n// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\ntype PluginConfig struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n\tName string\n\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n\tArgs runtime.Object\n}\n\n/*\n * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n */\nconst (\n\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n\n\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n\n\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\n\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n)\n\n// Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\nfunc (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n\tif p == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\textensions := []PluginSet{\n\t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n\t\tp.PreFilter,\n\t\tp.Filter,\n\t\tp.PostFilter,\n\t\tp.Reserve,\n\t\tp.PreScore,\n\t\tp.Score,\n\t\tp.PreBind,\n\t\tp.Bind,\n\t\tp.PostBind,\n\t\tp.Permit,\n\t\tp.QueueSort,\n\t}\n\tn := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, e := range extensions {\n\t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n\t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sets.List(n)\n}\n\n// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\ntype Extender struct {\n\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n\tURLPrefix string\n\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n\tFilterVerb string\n\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n\tPreemptVerb string\n\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n\tPrioritizeVerb string\n\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n\tWeight int64\n\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n\t// can implement this function.\n\tBindVerb string\n\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n\tEnableHTTPS bool\n\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\n\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n\tNodeCacheCapable bool\n\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n\t// this extender.\n\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n\t// +optional\n\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\n\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n\tIgnorable bool\n}\n\n// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n// managed by an extender.\ntype ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n\tName string\n\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n\t// resource when applying predicates.\n\tIgnoredByScheduler bool\n}\n\n// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\ntype ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n\tInsecure bool\n\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n\t// certificates against. In kube-scheduler, after\n\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\n\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\n\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\n\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\n\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\n\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\n\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta\n\n\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\n\tParallelism int32\n\n\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\n\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\n\n\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\n\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\n\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\n\n\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\n\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n\n\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\n\t// will be used.\n\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\n\n\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\n\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\n\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\n\n\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\n\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\n\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n\t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n\tProfiles []SchedulingProfile\n\n\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n\tExtenders []Extender\n\n\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n\t// Defaults to false.\n\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n}\n\n// SchedulingProfile is a scheduling profile.\ntype SchedulingProfile struct {\n\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n\tSchedulerName string\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n\n\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n\t// should be disabled.\n\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n\tPlugins *Plugins\n\n\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n\t// for that plugin.\n\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n}\n\n// KubeSchedulerProfile is an alias for SchedulingProfile for backwards compatibility.\n// Deprecated: Use SchedulingProfile instead.\ntype KubeSchedulerProfile = SchedulingProfile\n\n// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\ntype Plugins struct {\n\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n\n\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n\tQueueSort PluginSet\n\n\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\tPreFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n\tFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n\tPostFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n\tPreScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n\tScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n\tReserve PluginSet\n\n\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n\tPermit PluginSet\n\n\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n\tPreBind PluginSet\n\n\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n\tBind PluginSet\n\n\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n\tPostBind PluginSet\n\n\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n\tMultiPoint PluginSet\n}\n\n// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\ntype PluginSet struct {\n\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n\tEnabled []Plugin\n\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n\tDisabled []Plugin\n}\n\n// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\ntype Plugin struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n\tName string\n\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n\tWeight int32\n}\n\n// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\ntype PluginConfig struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n\tName string\n\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n\tArgs runtime.Object\n}\n\n/*\n * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n */\nconst (\n\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n\n\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n\n\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\n\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n)\n\n// Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\nfunc (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n\tif p == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\textensions := []PluginSet{\n\t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n\t\tp.PreFilter,\n\t\tp.Filter,\n\t\tp.PostFilter,\n\t\tp.Reserve,\n\t\tp.PreScore,\n\t\tp.Score,\n\t\tp.PreBind,\n\t\tp.Bind,\n\t\tp.PostBind,\n\t\tp.Permit,\n\t\tp.QueueSort,\n\t}\n\tn := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, e := range extensions {\n\t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n\t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sets.List(n)\n}\n\n// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\ntype Extender struct {\n\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n\tURLPrefix string\n\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n\tFilterVerb string\n\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n\tPreemptVerb string\n\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n\tPrioritizeVerb string\n\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n\tWeight int64\n\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. Only one extender\n\t// can implement this function.\n\tBindVerb string\n\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n\tEnableHTTPS bool\n\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. p\n\t}\n\treturn m, nil\n}\n\n// HandlesSchedulerName returns whether a profile handles the given scheduler name.\nfunc (m Map) HandlesSchedulerName(name string) bool {\n\t_, ok := m[name]\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// Close closes all frameworks registered in this map.\nfunc (m Map) Close() error {\n\tvar errs []error\n\tfor name, f := range m {\n\t\terr := f.Close()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"framework %s failed to close: %w\", name, err))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn errors.Join(errs...)\n}\n\n// NewRecorderFactory returns a RecorderFactory for the broadcaster.\nfunc NewRecorderFactory(b events.EventBroadcaster) RecorderFactory {\n\treturn func(name string) events.EventRecorder {\n\t\treturn b.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, name)\n\t}\n}\n\ntype cfgValidator struct {\n\tm             Map\n\tqueueSort     string\n\tqueueSortArgs runtime.Object\n}\n\nfunc (v *cfgValidator) validate(cfg config.SchedulingProfile, f framework.Framework) error {\n\tif len(f.ProfileName()) == 0 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"scheduler name is needed\")\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Plugins == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugins required for profile with scheduler name %q\", f.ProfileName())\n\t}\n\tif v.m[f.ProfileName()] != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate profile with scheduler name %q\", f.ProfileName())\n\t}\n\n\tqueueSort := f.ListPlugins().QueueSort.Enabled[0].Name\n\tvar queueSortArgs runtime.Object\n\tfor _, plCfg := range cfg.PluginConfig {\n\t\tif plCfg.Name == queueSort {\n\t\t\tqueueSortArgs = plCfg.Args\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(v.queueSort) == 0 {\n\t\tv.queueSort = queueSort\n\t\tv.queueSortArgs = queueSortArgs\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif v.queueSort != queueSort {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"different queue sort plugins for profile %q: %q, first: %q\", cfg.SchedulerName, queueSort, v.queueSort)\n\t}\n\tif !cmp.Equal(v.queueSortArgs, queueSortArgs) {\n\t\treturn
